narrative_findings: New Albany 16-court tournament-grade pickleball complex (7300 Walnut, opened 2023, free public, dawn-dusk). Plus 185,890 sf Fieldhouse & Community Center opening 2026.
New Albany trail mileage: 'over 50 miles' on official sources. The 80+ figure circulates in real-estate marketing but is NOT confirmed on city/parks sites.
Dublin Art in Public Places is the gold standard: est. 1988, 60+ works, 2021 Master Plan, funded by 6% hotel tax. Dublin Arts Council partnership.
DUBLIN HAS MUNICIPAL FIBER: DubLink 100 Gbps + 'Fiber to Every Home' substantially complete in 2026. Hudson Velocity is NOT unique in this benchmark.
Worthington Farmers Market is CONFIRMED year-round: outdoor summers in Old Worthington, indoor Nov-Apr at Shops at Worthington Place, est. 1987.
Worthington has FORMAL deer management with USDA federal sharpshooters Jan-Feb 2026.
Worthington's Griswold Center is a dedicated 55+ facility separate from the main Community Center.
Bexley Jeffrey Park / Jeffrey Mansion = 34 acres gifted by Jeffrey family in 1941; houses Recreation & Parks Dept, David H. Madison Community Pool, Bexley Historical Society.
Bexley Public Library is INDEPENDENT (separate from CML).
Bexley trash: alley pickup for alley properties (effectively rear-access for older grid).
Upper Arlington's Bob Crane Community Center: 165,000 sf, 5 floors, indoor pool, two gyms, walking track, art gallery, e-sports room, cafe. Top OPRA awards.
Upper Arlington Public Library is INDEPENDENT — established 1967 by Ohio HB 494.
UA Labor Day Arts Festival since 1966, 15K+ visitors, fireworks at Northam Park.

Powell: ~125 park acres / 10 parks. ~30 miles bike trails. Village Green hosts Powell Festival with fireworks. No municipal rec center. Del-Co Water + Delaware County Regional Sewer District. Delaware County District Library. Olentangy Local Schools.
caveats: Water/sewer per-5kgal rates: not captured (would require pulling each city's published rate ordinance).
Stormwater fees: not surfaced; most levy one but rates not published on homepage scans.
Powell stormwater/EV: WebFetch blocked (HTTP 403); marked null where unconfirmed.

amenities.parks_pools: 10 parks (~125 ac) including Village Green and Adventure Park; no municipal pool (relies on private/HOA).
amenities.trails: Nearly 30 miles of paved bike/multi-use trails connecting parks and neighborhoods.
amenities.pickleball: Courts at city parks; no dedicated complex.
amenities.dog_parks: Not a flagship; off-leash via county/township options.
amenities.library: Served by Delaware County District Library (independent county system).
amenities.senior_center: Programs only; no standalone senior facility.
amenities.farmers_market: Powell Chamber Farmers Market — Saturdays May-October at 240 N. Liberty St.
amenities.concert_series: Community Band Concert Series at Village Green in summer.
amenities.holiday_lighting: Holidays in Powell tree-lighting and seasonal events.
amenities.deer_management: No formal city-led culling program identified.
amenities.tree_program: Tree City USA; forestry via Public Works.
amenities.ev_chargers: Limited public EV; no documented municipal program.
amenities.downtown: Historic downtown around four corners (Olentangy/Liberty); Village Green civic core.

utilities.sewer: City of Bexley local; Columbus treatment

civic.rec_center_name: Jeffrey Mansion (Parks & Rec HQ, not a fitness center)

amenities.parks_pools: Jeffrey Park (34 ac, gifted 1941) anchors the system; David H. Madison Community Pool is the seasonal outdoor pool.
amenities.trails: Boardwalk along Alum Creek in Jeffrey Park; limited mileage due to dense first-ring grid.
amenities.pickleball: Court availability at parks; no dedicated complex.
amenities.dog_parks: No major dedicated dog park within city limits.
amenities.library: Bexley Public Library — fully independent single-branch system (separate from CML).
amenities.community_center: No purpose-built rec/community center; Jeffrey Mansion houses programming and admin.
amenities.senior_center: Dedicated Bexley Senior Center with programming.
amenities.public_art: Ad hoc + Capital University partnerships (Mees Hall, Schumacher Gallery); no formal master plan.
amenities.farmers_market: Bexley Farmers Market (seasonal, Cassady Ave / Capital University).
amenities.concert_series: Jazz in the Park summer series at Jeffrey Park.
amenities.holiday_lighting: Annual Tree Lighting + Light Collection winter display (Dec 1-22).
amenities.deer_management: No formal program identified on city site.
amenities.tree_program: Tree City USA; Earth & Arbor Month programming.
amenities.downtown: Main Street / Bexley Square along East Main — walkable district with shops and restaurants.

schools.note: Small, high-performing district with strong arts/academics

utilities.ev_chargers: Public EV at City facilities + Community Center area

civic.library: Worthington Libraries — INDEPENDENT multi-branch (Old Worthington, Northwest, Worthington Park)
civic.rec_center_name: Worthington Community Center (345 E. Wilson Bridge Rd)

amenities.parks_pools: 221+ acres; Worthington Community Center includes aquatics; outdoor pool program; McCord Park recently renovated.
amenities.trails: Olentangy River Parklands connect to regional Olentangy Trail; bike/multi-use network.
amenities.pickleball: Courts at parks + Community Center programming.
amenities.library: Worthington Libraries — independent system, nationally recognized, three branches.
amenities.community_center: Worthington Community Center — full-service rec with fitness, gym, aquatics.
amenities.senior_center: Griswold Center — dedicated 55+ facility (fitness, crafts, library, social).
amenities.public_art: Public art via Worthington Arts Council partnerships; not a Dublin-scale master plan.
amenities.farmers_market: Worthington Farmers Market — YEAR-ROUND (summer outdoor in Old Worthington / winter indoor Nov-Apr at Shops at Worthington Place); est. 1987.
amenities.concert_series: Summer concert series in Old Worthington (Village Green).
amenities.holiday_lighting: Old Worthington holiday lighting + Market Day events.
amenities.deer_management: Formal Deer Management Program; targeted removal by USDA Wildlife Services Jan-Feb 2026.
amenities.tree_program: Tree City USA; active urban forestry.
amenities.ev_chargers: Public EV at City facilities and Community Center.
amenities.downtown: Old Worthington along High Street — colonial-era walkable historic district anchored by Village Green.

civic.library: Columbus Metropolitan Library — New Albany Branch (200 Market St)
civic.rec_center_name: Philip Heit Center for Healthy New Albany (current) + Fieldhouse & Community Center (185,890 sf, opening 2026)
civic.senior_center: Programming via Healthy New Albany / Heit Center; no dedicated facility

amenities.parks_pools: Bevelhymer Park, Thompson Park, Wexner Park + extensive joint park district facilities; relies on private swim clubs (no municipal outdoor pool).
amenities.trails: Over 50 miles of paved leisure trails — the signature amenity, connecting neighborhoods, parks, and Village Center. ('80+' figure circulates but is NOT confirmed by official sources.)
amenities.pickleball: 16-court tournament-grade pickleball complex at 7300 Walnut St (opened 2023) — among the largest free public complexes in the Columbus region.
amenities.dog_parks: No flagship dog park identified in NA Parks system.
amenities.library: Columbus Metropolitan Library — New Albany Branch.
amenities.community_center: Philip Heit Center for Healthy New Albany (current) + 185,890 sf Fieldhouse & Community Center opening 2026 (indoor track, basketball, volleyball, pickleball).
amenities.senior_center: Programming via Healthy New Albany; no senior-only building.
amenities.public_art: Public art via Rose Run Park + New Albany Company partnerships; not Dublin-scale formal program.
amenities.farmers_market: Healthy New Albany Farmers Market — Thursdays July-August, Market Square.
amenities.concert_series: Summer Concert Series at Charleen & Charles Hinson Amphitheater.
amenities.holiday_lighting: Holiday lighting in Village Center / Market Square.
amenities.deer_management: No formal city-led culling program documented.
amenities.tree_program: Tree City USA; active community forestry.
amenities.ev_chargers: Public EV at municipal + corporate-campus locations; not centrally documented.
amenities.downtown: New Albany Village Center / Market Square — planned, Georgian-style walkable mixed-use core.

schools.note: Single unified K-12 campus; Amazon-sponsored 'Think Big' STEM corners

utilities.ev_chargers: Public EV at Municipal Services Center + Bob Crane CC

civic.library: Upper Arlington Public Library — INDEPENDENT, multi-branch (est. 1967 via Ohio HB 494)
civic.rec_center_name: Bob Crane Community Center (3200 Tremont Rd)
civic.senior_center: Through Bob Crane CC / Parks & Rec

amenities.parks_pools: Three outdoor pools (Memorial Day-Labor Day) + Bob Crane indoor aquatics; flagship parks: Northam, Thompson, Fancyburg, Reed Road.
amenities.trails: Tremont Road and neighborhood bike paths; integrated with Columbus regional trail system.
amenities.pickleball: Courts at Northam and other parks + indoor at Bob Crane CC.
amenities.dog_parks: Dog park within Parks & Rec system.
amenities.library: Upper Arlington Public Library — independent, multi-branch, locally governed since 1967.
amenities.community_center: Bob Crane Community Center: ~165,000 sf, 5 floors, indoor pool, two gyms, walking track, art gallery, e-sports room, cafe. Top OPRA awards.
amenities.senior_center: Senior programs through Bob Crane CC; no separate facility.
amenities.public_art: Public art at Northam, Miller Park; not Dublin-scale master plan.
amenities.farmers_market: Upper Arlington Farmers Market — Wednesdays 3-6 pm, May-September.
amenities.concert_series: UA Performance Series + Sounds of Summer at Mallway / Tremont Center.
amenities.holiday_lighting: Annual tree-lighting + holiday events at Mallway Park.
amenities.deer_management: Discussion ongoing; no formal sharpshooter program (vs. Worthington's).
amenities.tree_program: Tree City USA since 1990; annual Arbor Day planting.
amenities.ev_chargers: Public EV at Municipal Services Center + Bob Crane CC.
amenities.downtown: No traditional downtown; commercial nodes at Lane Ave, Kingsdale, Tremont/Arlington Ave.

schools.note: Single comprehensive HS district; nationally recognized academics

utilities.fiber_provider: DubLink (100 Gbps backbone) + Fiber to Every Home (FTTH, substantially complete 2026)
utilities.ev_chargers: Public EV at City facilities + Bridge Park

civic.senior_center: Programs through DCRC + Dublin Community Senior Citizens

amenities.parks_pools: 60+ parks including Bridge Park, Coffman Park, Glacier Ridge; Dublin Community Pool North + South for outdoor swimming.
amenities.trails: Over 130 miles of paved bike paths and shared-use trails — one of the largest networks in Ohio.
amenities.pickleball: Multiple pickleball locations across park system, including dedicated complexes.
amenities.dog_parks: Multiple dog parks (Coffman Park dog park, etc.).
amenities.community_center: Dublin Community Recreation Center (DCRC) — large multi-purpose with aquatics, gyms, theater.
amenities.public_art: Art in Public Places — established 1988, 60+ permanent works, 2021 Public Art Master Plan, funded by 6% hotel/motel tax, partnership with Dublin Arts Council.
amenities.concert_series: Sundays at Scioto + Bridge Park concerts.
amenities.holiday_lighting: Annual Tree Lighting at Bridge Park; year-round Bridge Street District lighting.
amenities.deer_management: Active deer management program (sharpshooter + habitat in parks).
amenities.tree_program: Tree City USA; robust urban forestry.
amenities.ev_chargers: Public EV at City facilities + Bridge Park garages.
amenities.downtown: Bridge Park (modern mixed-use along Riverside Dr) + Historic Dublin (across the Scioto) — dual downtown anchors.
